verses.kjv: And were all baptized unto Moses in the cloud and in the sea;
verses.bsb: They were all baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.

verses.esv: and all ate the same spiritual food,
verses.kjv: And did all eat the same spiritual meat;
verses.bsb: They all ate the same spiritual food

verses.esv: and all drank the same spiritual drink. For they drank from the spiritual Rock that followed them, and the Rock was Christ.
verses.kjv: And did all drink the same spiritual drink: for they drank of that spiritual Rock that followed them: and that Rock was Christ.
verses.bsb: and drank the same spiritual drink; for they drank from the spiritual rock that accompanied them, and that rock was Christ.

verses.esv: Nevertheless, with most of them God was not pleased, for they were overthrown in the wilderness.
verses.kjv: But with many of them God was not well pleased: for they were overthrown in the wilderness.
verses.bsb: Nevertheless, God was not pleased with most of them, for they were struck down in the wilderness.

verses.esv: Now these things took place as examples for us, that we might not desire evil as they did.
verses.kjv: Now these things were our examples, to the intent we should not lust after evil things, as they also lusted.
verses.bsb: These things took place as examples to keep us from craving evil things as they did.

verses.esv: Do not be idolaters as some of them were; as it is written, “The people sat down to eat and drink and rose up to play.”
verses.kjv: Neither be ye idolaters, as were some of them; as it is written, The people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to play.
verses.bsb: Do not be idolaters, as some of them were. As it is written: “The people sat down to eat and to drink, and got up to indulge in revelry.”

verses.esv: We must not indulge in sexual immorality as some of them did, and twenty-three thousand fell in a single day.
verses.kjv: Neither let us commit fornication, as some of them committed, and fell in one day three and twenty thousand.
verses.bsb: We should not commit sexual immorality, as some of them did, and in one day twenty-three thousand of them died.

verses.esv: We must not put Christ to the test, as some of them did and were destroyed by serpents,
verses.kjv: Neither let us tempt Christ, as some of them also tempted, and were destroyed of serpents.
verses.bsb: We should not test Christ, as some of them did, and were killed by snakes.

verses.esv: nor grumble, as some of them did and were destroyed by the Destroyer.
verses.kjv: Neither murmur ye, as some of them also murmured, and were destroyed of the destroyer.
verses.bsb: And do not complain, as some of them did, and were killed by the destroying angel.

verses.esv: Now these things happened to them as an example, but they were written down for our instruction, on whom the end of the ages has come.
verses.kjv: Now all these things happened unto them for ensamples: and they are written for our admonition, upon whom the ends of the world are come.
verses.bsb: Now these things happened to them as examples and were written down as warnings for us, on whom the fulfillment of the ages has come.

verses.esv: Therefore let anyone who thinks that he stands take heed lest he fall.
verses.kjv: Wherefore let him that thinketh he standeth take heed lest he fall.
verses.bsb: So the one who thinks he is standing firm should be careful not to fall.

verses.esv: No temptation has overtaken you that is not common to man. God is faithful, and he will not let you be tempted beyond your ability, but with the temptation he will also provide the way of escape, that you may be able to endure it.
verses.kjv: There hath no temptation taken you but such as is common to man: but God is faithful, who will not suffer you to be tempted above that ye are able; but will with the temptation also make a way to escape, that ye may be able to bear it.
verses.bsb: No temptation has seized you except what is common to man. And God is faithful; He will not let you be tempted beyond what you can bear. But when you are tempted, He will also provide an escape, so that you can stand up under it.

verses.esv: Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.
verses.kjv: Wherefore, my dearly beloved, flee from idolatry.
verses.bsb: Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.

verses.esv: I speak as to sensible people; judge for yourselves what I say.
verses.kjv: I speak as to wise men; judge ye what I say.
verses.bsb: I speak to reasonable people; judge for yourselves what I say.

verses.esv: The cup of blessing that we bless, is it not a participation in the blood of Christ? The bread that we break, is it not a participation in the body of Christ?
verses.kjv: The cup of blessing which we bless, is it not the communion of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, is it not the communion of the body of Christ?
verses.bsb: Is not the cup of blessing that we bless a participation in the blood of Christ? And is not the bread that we break a participation in the body of Christ?

verses.esv: Because there is one bread, we who are many are one body, for we all partake of the one bread.
verses.kjv: For we being many are one bread, and one body: for we are all partakers of that one bread.
verses.bsb: Because there is one loaf, we who are many are one body; for we all partake of the one loaf.

verses.esv: Consider the people of Israel: are not those who eat the sacrifices participants in the altar?
verses.kjv: Behold Israel after the flesh: are not they which eat of the sacrifices partakers of the altar?
verses.bsb: Consider the people of Israel: Are not those who eat the sacrifices fellow partakers in the altar?

verses.esv: What do I imply then? That food offered to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything?
verses.kjv: What say I then? that the idol is any thing, or that which is offered in sacrifice to idols is any thing?
verses.bsb: Am I suggesting, then, that food sacrificed to an idol is anything, or that an idol is anything?

verses.esv: No, I imply that what pagans sacrifice they offer to demons and not to God. I do not want you to be participants with demons.
verses.kjv: But I say, that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to devils, and not to God: and I would not that ye should have fellowship with devils.
verses.bsb: No, but the sacrifices of pagans are offered to demons, not to God. And I do not want you to be participants with demons.

verses.esv: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You cannot partake of the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
verses.kjv: Ye cannot drink the cup of the Lord, and the cup of devils: ye cannot be partakers of the Lord’s table, and of the table of devils.
verses.bsb: You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons too; you cannot partake in the table of the Lord and the table of demons too.

verses.esv: Shall we provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than he?
verses.kjv: Do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? are we stronger than he?
verses.bsb: Are we trying to provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than He?

verses.esv: “All things are lawful,” but not all things are helpful. “All things are lawful,” but not all things build up.
verses.kjv: All things are lawful for me, but all things are not expedient: all things are lawful for me, but all things edify not.
verses.bsb: “Everything is permissible,” but not everything is beneficial. “Everything is permissible,” but not everything is edifying.

verses.esv: Let no one seek his own good, but the good of his neighbor.
verses.kjv: Let no man seek his own, but every man another’s wealth.
verses.bsb: No one should seek his own good, but the good of others.

verses.esv: Eat whatever is sold in the meat market without raising any question on the ground of conscience.
verses.kjv: Whatsoever is sold in the shambles, that eat, asking no question for conscience sake:
verses.bsb: Eat anything sold in the meat market without raising questions of conscience,

verses.esv: For “the earth is the Lord's, and the fullness thereof.”
verses.kjv: For the earth is the Lord’s, and the fulness thereof.
verses.bsb: for, “The earth is the Lord’s, and the fullness thereof.”

verses.esv: If one of the unbelievers invites you to dinner and you are disposed to go, eat whatever is set before you without raising any question on the ground of conscience.
verses.kjv: If any of them that believe not bid you to a feast, and ye be disposed to go; whatsoever is set before you, eat, asking no question for conscience sake.
verses.bsb: If an unbeliever invites you to a meal and you want to go, eat anything set before you without raising questions of conscience.

verses.esv: But if someone says to you, “This has been offered in sacrifice,” then do not eat it, for the sake of the one who informed you, and for the sake of conscience—
verses.kjv: But if any man say unto you, This is offered in sacrifice unto idols, eat not for his sake that shewed it, and for conscience sake: for the earth is the Lord’s, and the fulness thereof:
verses.bsb: But if someone tells you, “This food was offered to idols,” then do not eat it, for the sake of the one who told you and for the sake of conscience—

verses.esv: I do not mean your conscience, but his. For why should my liberty be determined by someone else's conscience?
verses.kjv: Conscience, I say, not thine own, but of the other: for why is my liberty judged of another man’s conscience?
verses.bsb: the other one’s conscience, I mean, not your own. For why should my freedom be determined by someone else’s conscience?

verses.esv: If I partake with thankfulness, why am I denounced because of that for which I give thanks?
verses.kjv: For if I by grace be a partaker, why am I evil spoken of for that for which I give thanks?
verses.bsb: If I partake in the meal with thankfulness, why am I denounced because of that for which I give thanks?

verses.esv: So, whether you eat or drink, or whatever you do, do all to the glory of God.
verses.kjv: Whether therefore ye eat, or drink, or whatsoever ye do, do all to the glory of God.
verses.bsb: So whether you eat or drink or whatever you do, do it all to the glory of God.

verses.esv: Give no offense to Jews or to Greeks or to the church of God,
verses.kjv: Give none offence, neither to the Jews, nor to the Gentiles, nor to the church of God:
verses.bsb: Do not become a stumbling block, whether to Jews or Greeks or the church of God—

verses.esv: just as I try to please everyone in everything I do, not seeking my own advantage, but that of many, that they may be saved. Be imitators of me, as I am of Christ.
verses.kjv: Even as I please all men in all things, not seeking mine own profit, but the profit of many, that they may be saved.
verses.bsb: as I also try to please everyone in all I do. For I am not seeking my own good, but the good of many, that they may be saved.

verses.esv: Now I commend you because you remember me in everything and maintain the traditions even as I delivered them to you.
verses.kjv: Now I praise you, brethren, that ye remember me in all things, and keep the ordinances, as I delivered them to you.
verses.bsb: Now I commend you for remembering me in everything and for maintaining the traditions, just as I passed them on to you.

verses.esv: But I want you to understand that the head of every man is Christ, the head of a wife is her husband, and the head of Christ is God.
verses.kjv: But I would have you know, that the head of every man is Christ; and the head of the woman is the man; and the head of Christ is God.
verses.bsb: But I want you to understand that the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is man, and the head of Christ is God.

verses.esv: Every man who prays or prophesies with his head covered dishonors his head,
verses.kjv: Every man praying or prophesying, having his head covered, dishonoureth his head.
verses.bsb: Every man who prays or prophesies with his head covered dishonors his head.

verses.esv: but every wife who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered dishonors her head, since it is the same as if her head were shaven.
verses.kjv: But every woman that prayeth or prophesieth with her head uncovered dishonoureth her head: for that is even all one as if she were shaven.
verses.bsb: And every woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered dishonors her head, for it is just as if her head were shaved.

verses.esv: For if a wife will not cover her head, then she should cut her hair short. But since it is disgraceful for a wife to cut off her hair or shave her head, let her cover her head.
verses.kjv: For if the woman be not covered, let her also be shorn: but if it be a shame for a woman to be shorn or shaven, let her be covered.
verses.bsb: If a woman does not cover her head, she should have her hair cut off. And if it is shameful for a woman to have her hair cut or shaved off, she should cover her head.

verses.esv: For a man ought not to cover his head, since he is the image and glory of God, but woman is the glory of man.
verses.kjv: For a man indeed ought not to cover his head, forasmuch as he is the image and glory of God: but the woman is the glory of the man.
verses.bsb: A man ought not to cover his head, since he is the image and glory of God; but the woman is the glory of man.

verses.esv: For man was not made from woman, but woman from man.
verses.kjv: For the man is not of the woman; but the woman of the man.
verses.bsb: For man did not come from woman, but woman from man.

verses.esv: Neither was man created for woman, but woman for man.
verses.kjv: Neither was the man created for the woman; but the woman for the man.
verses.bsb: Neither was man created for woman, but woman for man.

verses.esv: That is why a wife ought to have a symbol of authority on her head, because of the angels.
verses.kjv: For this cause ought the woman to have power on her head because of the angels.
verses.bsb: For this reason a woman ought to have a sign of authority on her head, because of the angels.

verses.esv: Nevertheless, in the Lord woman is not independent of man nor man of woman;
verses.kjv: Nevertheless neither is the man without the woman, neither the woman without the man, in the Lord.
verses.bsb: In the Lord, however, woman is not independent of man, nor is man independent of woman.

verses.esv: for as woman was made from man, so man is now born of woman. And all things are from God.
verses.kjv: For as the woman is of the man, even so is the man also by the woman; but all things of God.
verses.bsb: For just as woman came from man, so also man is born of woman. But everything comes from God.

verses.esv: Judge for yourselves: is it proper for a wife to pray to God with her head uncovered?
verses.kjv: Judge in yourselves: is it comely that a woman pray unto God uncovered?
verses.bsb: Judge for yourselves: Is it proper for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?

verses.esv: Does not nature itself teach you that if a man wears long hair it is a disgrace for him,
verses.kjv: Doth not even nature itself teach you, that, if a man have long hair, it is a shame unto him?
verses.bsb: Doesn’t nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a disgrace to him,

verses.esv: but if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? For her hair is given to her for a covering.
verses.kjv: But if a woman have long hair, it is a glory to her: for her hair is given her for a covering.
verses.bsb: but that if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? For long hair is given to her as a covering.

verses.esv: If anyone is inclined to be contentious, we have no such practice, nor do the churches of God.
verses.kjv: But if any man seem to be contentious, we have no such custom, neither the churches of God.
verses.bsb: If anyone is inclined to dispute this, we have no other practice, nor do the churches of God.

verses.esv: But in the following instructions I do not commend you, because when you come together it is not for the better but for the worse.
verses.kjv: Now in this that I declare unto you I praise you not, that ye come together not for the better, but for the worse.
verses.bsb: In the following instructions I have no praise to offer, because your gatherings do more harm than good.

verses.esv: For, in the first place, when you come together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you. And I believe it in part,
verses.kjv: For first of all, when ye come together in the church, I hear that there be divisions among you; and I partly believe it.
verses.bsb: First of all, I hear that when you come together as a church, there are divisions among you, and in part I believe it.

verses.esv: for there must be factions among you in order that those who are genuine among you may be recognized.
verses.kjv: For there must be also heresies among you, that they which are approved may be made manifest among you.
verses.bsb: And indeed, there must be differences among you to show which of you are approved.

verses.esv: When you come together, it is not the Lord's supper that you eat.
verses.kjv: When ye come together therefore into one place, this is not to eat the Lord’s supper.
verses.bsb: Now then, when you come together, it is not the Lord’s Supper you eat.

verses.esv: For in eating, each one goes ahead with his own meal. One goes hungry, another gets drunk.
verses.kjv: For in eating every one taketh before other his own supper: and one is hungry, and another is drunken.
verses.bsb: For as you eat, each of you goes ahead without sharing his meal. While one remains hungry, another gets drunk.

verses.esv: What! Do you not have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you despise the church of God and humiliate those who have nothing? What shall I say to you? Shall I commend you in this? No, I will not.
verses.kjv: What? have ye not houses to eat and to drink in? or despise ye the church of God, and shame them that have not? What shall I say to you? shall I praise you in this? I praise you not.
verses.bsb: Don’t you have your own homes in which to eat and drink? Or do you despise the church of God and humiliate those who have nothing? What can I say to you? Shall I praise you for this? No, I will not!

verses.esv: For I received from the Lord what I also delivered to you, that the Lord Jesus on the night when he was betrayed took bread,
verses.kjv: For I have received of the Lord that which also I delivered unto you, That the Lord Jesus the same night in which he was betrayed took bread:
verses.bsb: For I received from the Lord what I also passed on to you: The Lord Jesus, on the night He was betrayed, took bread,

verses.esv: and when he had given thanks, he broke it, and said, “This is my body, which is for you. Do this in remembrance of me.”
verses.kjv: And when he had given thanks, he brake it, and said, Take, eat: this is my body, which is broken for you: this do in remembrance of me.
verses.bsb: and when He had given thanks, He broke it and said, “This is My body, which is for you; do this in remembrance of Me.”

verses.esv: In the same way also he took the cup, after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Do this, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of me.”
verses.kjv: After the same manner also he took the cup, when he had supped, saying, This cup is the new testament in my blood: this do ye, as oft as ye drink it, in remembrance of me.
verses.bsb: In the same way, after supper He took the cup, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in My blood; do this, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of Me.”

verses.esv: For as often as you eat this bread and drink the cup, you proclaim the Lord's death until he comes.
verses.kjv: For as often as ye eat this bread, and drink this cup, ye do shew the Lord’s death till he come.
verses.bsb: For as often as you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death until He comes.

verses.esv: Whoever, therefore, eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be guilty concerning the body and blood of the Lord.
verses.kjv: Wherefore whosoever shall eat this bread, and drink this cup of the Lord, unworthily, shall be guilty of the body and blood of the Lord.
verses.bsb: Therefore, whoever eats the bread or drinks the cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner will be guilty of sinning against the body and blood of the Lord.

verses.esv: Let a person examine himself, then, and so eat of the bread and drink of the cup.
verses.kjv: But let a man examine himself, and so let him eat of that bread, and drink of that cup.
verses.bsb: Each one must examine himself before he eats of the bread and drinks of the cup.

verses.esv: For anyone who eats and drinks without discerning the body eats and drinks judgment on himself.
verses.kjv: For he that eateth and drinketh unworthily, eateth and drinketh damnation to himself, not discerning the Lord’s body.
verses.bsb: For anyone who eats and drinks without recognizing the body eats and drinks judgment on himself.

verses.esv: That is why many of you are weak and ill, and some have died.
verses.kjv: For this cause many are weak and sickly among you, and many sleep.
verses.bsb: That is why many among you are weak and sick, and a number of you have fallen asleep.

verses.esv: But if we judged ourselves truly, we would not be judged.
verses.kjv: For if we would judge ourselves, we should not be judged.
verses.bsb: Now if we judged ourselves properly, we would not come under judgment.

verses.esv: But when we are judged by the Lord, we are disciplined so that we may not be condemned along with the world.
verses.kjv: But when we are judged, we are chastened of the Lord, that we should not be condemned with the world.
verses.bsb: But when we are judged by the Lord, we are being disciplined so that we will not be condemned with the world.

verses.esv: So then, my brothers, when you come together to eat, wait for one another—
verses.kjv: Wherefore, my brethren, when ye come together to eat, tarry one for another.
verses.bsb: So, my brothers, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.

verses.esv: if anyone is hungry, let him eat at home—so that when you come together it will not be for judgment. About the other things I will give directions when I come.
verses.kjv: And if any man hunger, let him eat at home; that ye come not together unto condemnation. And the rest will I set in order when I come.
verses.bsb: If anyone is hungry, he should eat at home, so that when you come together it will not result in judgment. And when I come, I will give instructions about the remaining matters.

verses.esv: Now concerning spiritual gifts, brothers, I do not want you to be uninformed.
verses.kjv: NOW concerning spiritual gifts, brethren, I would not have you ignorant.
verses.bsb: Now about spiritual gifts, brothers, I do not want you to be uninformed.

verses.esv: You know that when you were pagans you were led astray to mute idols, however you were led.
verses.kjv: Ye know that ye were Gentiles, carried away unto these dumb idols, even as ye were led.
verses.bsb: You know that when you were pagans, you were influenced and led astray to mute idols.

verses.esv: Therefore I want you to understand that no one speaking in the Spirit of God ever says “Jesus is accursed!” and no one can say “Jesus is Lord” except in the Holy Spirit.
verses.kjv: Wherefore I give you to understand, that no man speaking by the Spirit of God calleth Jesus accursed: and that no man can say that Jesus is the Lord, but by the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: Therefore I inform you that no one who is speaking by the Spirit of God says, “Jesus be cursed,” and no one can say, “Jesus is Lord,” except by the Holy Spirit.

verses.esv: Now there are varieties of gifts, but the same Spirit;
verses.kjv: Now there are diversities of gifts, but the same Spirit.
verses.bsb: There are different gifts, but the same Spirit.

verses.esv: and there are varieties of service, but the same Lord;
verses.kjv: And there are differences of administrations, but the same Lord.
verses.bsb: There are different ministries, but the same Lord.

verses.esv: and there are varieties of activities, but it is the same God who empowers them all in everyone.
verses.kjv: And there are diversities of operations, but it is the same God which worketh all in all.
verses.bsb: There are different ways of working, but the same God works all things in all people.

verses.esv: To each is given the manifestation of the Spirit for the common good.
verses.kjv: But the manifestation of the Spirit is given to every man to profit withal.
verses.bsb: Now to each one the manifestation of the Spirit is given for the common good.

verses.esv: For to one is given through the Spirit the utterance of wisdom, and to another the utterance of knowledge according to the same Spirit,
verses.kjv: For to one is given by the Spirit the word of wisdom; to another the word of knowledge by the same Spirit;
verses.bsb: To one there is given through the Spirit the message of wisdom, to another the message of knowledge by the same Spirit,

verses.esv: to another faith by the same Spirit, to another gifts of healing by the one Spirit,
verses.kjv: To another faith by the same Spirit; to another the gifts of healing by the same Spirit;
verses.bsb: to another faith by the same Spirit, to another gifts of healing by that one Spirit,

verses.esv: to another the working of miracles, to another prophecy, to another the ability to distinguish between spirits, to another various kinds of tongues, to another the interpretation of tongues.
verses.kjv: To another the working of miracles; to another prophecy; to another discerning of spirits; to another divers kinds of tongues; to another the interpretation of tongues:
verses.bsb: to another the working of miracles, to another prophecy, to another distinguishing between spirits, to another speaking in various tongues, and to still another the interpretation of tongues.

verses.esv: All these are empowered by one and the same Spirit, who apportions to each one individually as he wills.
verses.kjv: But all these worketh that one and the selfsame Spirit, dividing to every man severally as he will.
verses.bsb: All these are the work of one and the same Spirit, who apportions them to each one as He determines.

verses.esv: For just as the body is one and has many members, and all the members of the body, though many, are one body, so it is with Christ.
verses.kjv: For as the body is one, and hath many members, and all the members of that one body, being many, are one body: so also is Christ.
verses.bsb: The body is a unit, though it is composed of many parts. And although its parts are many, they all form one body. So it is with Christ.

verses.esv: For in one Spirit we were all baptized into one body—Jews or Greeks, slaves or free—and all were made to drink of one Spirit.
verses.kjv: For by one Spirit are we all baptized into one body, whether we be Jews or Gentiles, whether we be bond or free; and have been all made to drink into one Spirit.
verses.bsb: For in one Spirit we were all baptized into one body, whether Jews or Greeks, slave or free, and we were all given one Spirit to drink.

verses.esv: For the body does not consist of one member but of many.
verses.kjv: For the body is not one member, but many.
verses.bsb: For the body does not consist of one part, but of many.

verses.esv: If the foot should say, “Because I am not a hand, I do not belong to the body,” that would not make it any less a part of the body.
verses.kjv: If the foot shall say, Because I am not the hand, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?
verses.bsb: If the foot should say, “Because I am not a hand, I do not belong to the body,” that would not make it any less a part of the body.

verses.esv: And if the ear should say, “Because I am not an eye, I do not belong to the body,” that would not make it any less a part of the body.
verses.kjv: And if the ear shall say, Because I am not the eye, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?
verses.bsb: And if the ear should say, “Because I am not an eye, I do not belong to the body,” that would not make it any less a part of the body.

verses.esv: If the whole body were an eye, where would be the sense of hearing? If the whole body were an ear, where would be the sense of smell?
verses.kjv: If the whole body were an eye, where were the hearing? If the whole were hearing, where were the smelling?
verses.bsb: If the whole body were an eye, where would the sense of hearing be? If the whole body were an ear, where would the sense of smell be?

verses.esv: But as it is, God arranged the members in the body, each one of them, as he chose.
verses.kjv: But now hath God set the members every one of them in the body, as it hath pleased him.
verses.bsb: But in fact, God has arranged the members of the body, every one of them, according to His design.

verses.esv: If all were a single member, where would the body be?
verses.kjv: And if they were all one member, where were the body?
verses.bsb: If they were all one part, where would the body be?

verses.esv: As it is, there are many parts, yet one body.
verses.kjv: But now are they many members, yet but one body.
verses.bsb: As it is, there are many parts, but one body.

verses.esv: The eye cannot say to the hand, “I have no need of you,” nor again the head to the feet, “I have no need of you.”
verses.kjv: And the eye cannot say unto the hand, I have no need of thee: nor again the head to the feet, I have no need of you.
verses.bsb: The eye cannot say to the hand, “I do not need you.” Nor can the head say to the feet, “I do not need you.”

verses.esv: On the contrary, the parts of the body that seem to be weaker are indispensable,
verses.kjv: Nay, much more those members of the body, which seem to be more feeble, are necessary:
verses.bsb: On the contrary, the parts of the body that seem to be weaker are indispensable,

verses.esv: and on those parts of the body that we think less honorable we bestow the greater honor, and our unpresentable parts are treated with greater modesty,
verses.kjv: And those members of the body, which we think to be less honourable, upon these we bestow more abundant honour; and our uncomely parts have more abundant comeliness.
verses.bsb: and the parts we consider less honorable, we treat with greater honor. And our unpresentable parts are treated with special modesty,

verses.esv: which our more presentable parts do not require. But God has so composed the body, giving greater honor to the part that lacked it,
verses.kjv: For our comely parts have no need: but God hath tempered the body together, having given more abundant honour to that part which lacked:
verses.bsb: whereas our presentable parts have no such need.  But God has composed the body and has given greater honor to the parts that lacked it,

verses.esv: that there may be no division in the body, but that the members may have the same care for one another.
verses.kjv: That there should be no schism in the body; but that the members should have the same care one for another.
verses.bsb: so that there should be no division in the body, but that its members should have mutual concern for one another.

verses.esv: If one member suffers, all suffer together; if one member is honored, all rejoice together.
verses.kjv: And whether one member suffer, all the members suffer with it; or one member be honoured, all the members rejoice with it.
verses.bsb: If one part suffers, every part suffers with it; if one part is honored, every part rejoices with it.

verses.esv: Now you are the body of Christ and individually members of it.
verses.kjv: Now ye are the body of Christ, and members in particular.
verses.bsb: Now you are the body of Christ, and each of you is a member of it.

verses.esv: And God has appointed in the church first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healing, helping, administrating, and various kinds of tongues.
verses.kjv: And God hath set some in the church, first apostles, secondarily prophets, thirdly teachers, after that miracles, then gifts of healings, helps, governments, diversities of tongues.
verses.bsb: And in the church God has appointed first of all apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then workers of miracles, and those with gifts of healing, helping, administration, and various tongues.

verses.esv: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Do all work miracles?
verses.kjv: Are all apostles? are all prophets? are all teachers? are all workers of miracles?
verses.bsb: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Do all work miracles?

verses.esv: Do all possess gifts of healing? Do all speak with tongues? Do all interpret?
verses.kjv: Have all the gifts of healing? do all speak with tongues? do all interpret?
verses.bsb: Do all have gifts of healing? Do all speak in tongues? Do all interpret?

verses.esv: But earnestly desire the higher gifts.
verses.kjv: But covet earnestly the best gifts: and yet shew I unto you a more excellent way.
verses.bsb: But eagerly desire the greater gifts.  And now I will show you the most excellent way.

verses.esv: If I speak in the tongues of men and of angels, but have not love, I am a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
verses.kjv: THOUGH I speak with the tongues of men and of angels, and have not charity, I am become as sounding brass, or a tinkling cymbal.
verses.bsb: If I speak in the tongues of men and of angels, but have not love, I am only a ringing gong or a clanging cymbal.

verses.esv: And if I have prophetic powers, and understand all mysteries and all knowledge, and if I have all faith, so as to remove mountains, but have not love, I am nothing.
verses.kjv: And though I have the gift of prophecy, and understand all mysteries, and all knowledge; and though I have all faith, so that I could remove mountains, and have not charity, I am nothing.
verses.bsb: If I have the gift of prophecy and can fathom all mysteries and all knowledge, and if I have absolute faith so as to move mountains, but have not love, I am nothing.

verses.esv: If I give away all I have, and if I deliver up my body to be burned, but have not love, I gain nothing.
verses.kjv: And though I bestow all my goods to feed the poor, and though I give my body to be burned, and have not charity, it profiteth me nothing.
verses.bsb: If I give all I possess to the poor and exult in the surrender of my body, but have not love, I gain nothing.

verses.esv: Love is patient and kind; love does not envy or boast; it is not arrogant
verses.kjv: Charity suffereth long, and is kind; charity envieth not; charity vaunteth not itself, is not puffed up,
verses.bsb: Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud.

verses.esv: or rude. It does not insist on its own way; it is not irritable or resentful;
verses.kjv: Doth not behave itself unseemly, seeketh not her own, is not easily provoked, thinketh no evil;
verses.bsb: It is not rude, it is not self-seeking, it is not easily angered, it keeps no account of wrongs.

verses.esv: it does not rejoice at wrongdoing, but rejoices with the truth.
verses.kjv: Rejoiceth not in iniquity, but rejoiceth in the truth;
verses.bsb: Love takes no pleasure in evil, but rejoices in the truth.

verses.esv: Love bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
verses.kjv: Beareth all things, believeth all things, hopeth all things, endureth all things.
verses.bsb: It bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.

verses.esv: Love never ends. As for prophecies, they will pass away; as for tongues, they will cease; as for knowledge, it will pass away.
verses.kjv: Charity never faileth: but whether there be prophecies, they shall fail; whether there be tongues, they shall cease; whether there be knowledge, it shall vanish away.
verses.bsb: Love never fails. But where there are prophecies, they will cease; where there are tongues, they will be restrained; where there is knowledge, it will be dismissed.

verses.esv: For we know in part and we prophesy in part,
verses.kjv: For we know in part, and we prophesy in part.
verses.bsb: For we know in part and we prophesy in part,

verses.esv: but when the perfect comes, the partial will pass away.
verses.kjv: But when that which is perfect is come, then that which is in part shall be done away.
verses.bsb: but when the perfect comes, the partial passes away.

verses.esv: When I was a child, I spoke like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child. When I became a man, I gave up childish ways.
verses.kjv: When I was a child, I spake as a child, I understood as a child, I thought as a child: but when I became a man, I put away childish things.
verses.bsb: When I was a child, I talked like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child. When I became a man, I set aside childish ways.

verses.esv: For now we see in a mirror dimly, but then face to face. Now I know in part; then I shall know fully, even as I have been fully known.
verses.kjv: For now we see through a glass, darkly; but then face to face: now I know in part; but then shall I know even as also I am known.
verses.bsb: Now we see but a dim reflection as in a mirror; then we shall see face to face. Now I know in part; then I shall know fully, even as I am fully known.

verses.esv: So now faith, hope, and love abide, these three; but the greatest of these is love.
verses.kjv: And now abideth faith, hope, charity, these three; but the greatest of these is charity.
verses.bsb: And now these three remain: faith, hope, and love; but the greatest of these is love.

verses.esv: Pursue love, and earnestly desire the spiritual gifts, especially that you may prophesy.
verses.kjv: FOLLOW after charity, and desire spiritual gifts, but rather that ye may prophesy.
verses.bsb: Earnestly pursue love and eagerly desire spiritual gifts, especially the gift of prophecy.

verses.esv: For one who speaks in a tongue speaks not to men but to God; for no one understands him, but he utters mysteries in the Spirit.
verses.kjv: For he that speaketh in an unknown tongue speaketh not unto men, but unto God: for no man understandeth him; howbeit in the spirit he speaketh mysteries.
verses.bsb: For he who speaks in a tongue does not speak to men, but to God. Indeed, no one understands him; he utters mysteries in the Spirit.

verses.esv: On the other hand, the one who prophesies speaks to people for their upbuilding and encouragement and consolation.
verses.kjv: But he that prophesieth speaketh unto men to edification, and exhortation, and comfort.
verses.bsb: But he who prophesies speaks to men for their edification, encouragement, and comfort.

verses.esv: The one who speaks in a tongue builds up himself, but the one who prophesies builds up the church.
verses.kjv: He that speaketh in an unknown tongue edifieth himself; but he that prophesieth edifieth the church.
verses.bsb: The one who speaks in a tongue edifies himself, but the one who prophesies edifies the church.

verses.esv: Now I want you all to speak in tongues, but even more to prophesy. The one who prophesies is greater than the one who speaks in tongues, unless someone interprets, so that the church may be built up.
verses.kjv: I would that ye all spake with tongues, but rather that ye prophesied: for greater is he that prophesieth than he that speaketh with tongues, except he interpret, that the church may receive edifying.
verses.bsb: I wish that all of you could speak in tongues, but I would rather have you prophesy. He who prophesies is greater than one who speaks in tongues, unless he interprets so that the church may be edified.

verses.esv: Now, brothers, if I come to you speaking in tongues, how will I benefit you unless I bring you some revelation or knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
verses.kjv: Now, brethren, if I come unto you speaking with tongues, what shall I profit you, except I shall speak to you either by revelation, or by knowledge, or by prophesying, or by doctrine?
verses.bsb: Now, brothers, if I come to you speaking in tongues, how will I benefit you, unless I bring you some revelation or knowledge or prophecy or teaching?

verses.esv: If even lifeless instruments, such as the flute or the harp, do not give distinct notes, how will anyone know what is played?
verses.kjv: And even things without life giving sound, whether pipe or harp, except they give a distinction in the sounds, how shall it be known what is piped or harped?
verses.bsb: Even in the case of lifeless instruments, such as the flute or harp, how will anyone recognize the tune they are playing unless the notes are distinct?

verses.esv: And if the bugle gives an indistinct sound, who will get ready for battle?
verses.kjv: For if the trumpet give an uncertain sound, who shall prepare himself to the battle?
verses.bsb: Again, if the trumpet sounds a muffled call, who will prepare for battle?

verses.esv: So with yourselves, if with your tongue you utter speech that is not intelligible, how will anyone know what is said? For you will be speaking into the air.
verses.kjv: So likewise ye, except ye utter by the tongue words easy to be understood, how shall it be known what is spoken? for ye shall speak into the air.
verses.bsb: So it is with you. Unless you speak intelligible words with your tongue, how will anyone know what you are saying? You will just be speaking into the air.

verses.esv: There are doubtless many different languages in the world, and none is without meaning,
verses.kjv: There are, it may be, so many kinds of voices in the world, and none of them is without signification.
verses.bsb: Assuredly, there are many different languages in the world, yet none of them is without meaning.

verses.esv: but if I do not know the meaning of the language, I will be a foreigner to the speaker and the speaker a foreigner to me.
verses.kjv: Therefore if I know not the meaning of the voice, I shall be unto him that speaketh a barbarian, and he that speaketh shall be a barbarian unto me.
verses.bsb: If, then, I do not know the meaning of someone’s language, I am a foreigner to the speaker, and he is a foreigner to me.

verses.esv: So with yourselves, since you are eager for manifestations of the Spirit, strive to excel in building up the church.
verses.kjv: Even so ye, forasmuch as ye are zealous of spiritual gifts, seek that ye may excel to the edifying of the church.
verses.bsb: It is the same with you. Since you are eager to have spiritual gifts, strive to excel in gifts that build up the church.

verses.esv: Therefore, one who speaks in a tongue should pray that he may interpret.
verses.kjv: Wherefore let him that speaketh in an unknown tongue pray that he may interpret.
verses.bsb: Therefore, the one who speaks in a tongue should pray that he may interpret.

verses.esv: For if I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays but my mind is unfruitful.
verses.kjv: For if I pray in an unknown tongue, my spirit prayeth, but my understanding is unfruitful.
verses.bsb: For if I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays, but my mind is unfruitful.

verses.esv: What am I to do? I will pray with my spirit, but I will pray with my mind also; I will sing praise with my spirit, but I will sing with my mind also.
verses.kjv: What is it then? I will pray with the spirit, and I will pray with the understanding also: I will sing with the spirit, and I will sing with the understanding also.
verses.bsb: What then shall I do? I will pray with my spirit, but I will also pray with my mind. I will sing with my spirit, but I will also sing with my mind.

verses.esv: Otherwise, if you give thanks with your spirit, how can anyone in the position of an outsider say “Amen” to your thanksgiving when he does not know what you are saying?
verses.kjv: Else when thou shalt bless with the spirit, how shall he that occupieth the room of the unlearned say Amen at thy giving of thanks, seeing he understandeth not what thou sayest?
verses.bsb: Otherwise, if you speak a blessing in spirit, how can someone who is uninstructed say “Amen” to your thanksgiving, since he does not know what you are saying?

verses.esv: For you may be giving thanks well enough, but the other person is not being built up.
verses.kjv: For thou verily givest thanks well, but the other is not edified.
verses.bsb: You may be giving thanks well enough, but the other one is not edified.

verses.esv: I thank God that I speak in tongues more than all of you.
verses.kjv: I thank my God, I speak with tongues more than ye all:
verses.bsb: I thank God that I speak in tongues more than all of you.

verses.esv: Nevertheless, in church I would rather speak five words with my mind in order to instruct others, than ten thousand words in a tongue.
verses.kjv: Yet in the church I had rather speak five words with my understanding, that by my voice I might teach others also, than ten thousand words in an unknown tongue.
verses.bsb: But in the church, I would rather speak five coherent words to instruct others than ten thousand words in a tongue.

verses.esv: Brothers, do not be children in your thinking. Be infants in evil, but in your thinking be mature.
verses.kjv: Brethren, be not children in understanding: howbeit in malice be ye children, but in understanding be men.
verses.bsb: Brothers, stop thinking like children. In regard to evil be infants, but in your thinking be mature.

verses.esv: In the Law it is written, “By people of strange tongues and by the lips of foreigners will I speak to this people, and even then they will not listen to me, says the Lord.”
verses.kjv: In the law it is written, With men of other tongues and other lips will I speak unto this people; and yet for all that will they not hear me, saith the Lord.
verses.bsb: It is written in the Law:  “By strange tongues and foreign lips I will speak to this people, but even then they will not listen to Me, says the Lord.”

verses.esv: Thus tongues are a sign not for believers but for unbelievers, while prophecy is a sign not for unbelievers but for believers.
verses.kjv: Wherefore tongues are for a sign, not to them that believe, but to them that believe not: but prophesying serveth not for them that believe not, but for them which believe.
verses.bsb: Tongues, then, are a sign, not for believers, but for unbelievers. Prophecy, however, is for believers, not for unbelievers.

verses.esv: If, therefore, the whole church comes together and all speak in tongues, and outsiders or unbelievers enter, will they not say that you are out of your minds?
verses.kjv: If therefore the whole church be come together into one place, and all speak with tongues, and there come in those that are unlearned, or unbelievers, will they not say that ye are mad?
verses.bsb: So if the whole church comes together and everyone speaks in tongues, and some who are uninstructed or some unbelievers come in, will they not say that you are out of your minds?

verses.esv: But if all prophesy, and an unbeliever or outsider enters, he is convicted by all, he is called to account by all,
verses.kjv: But if all prophesy, and there come in one that believeth not, or one unlearned, he is convinced of all, he is judged of all:
verses.bsb: But if an unbeliever or uninstructed person comes in while everyone is prophesying, he will be convicted and called to account by all,

verses.esv: the secrets of his heart are disclosed, and so, falling on his face, he will worship God and declare that God is really among you.
verses.kjv: And thus are the secrets of his heart made manifest; and so falling down on his face he will worship God, and report that God is in you of a truth.
verses.bsb: and the secrets of his heart will be made known. So he will fall facedown and worship God, proclaiming, “God is truly among you!”

verses.esv: What then, brothers? When you come together, each one has a hymn, a lesson, a revelation, a tongue, or an interpretation. Let all things be done for building up.
verses.kjv: How is it then, brethren? when ye come together, every one of you hath a psalm, hath a doctrine, hath a tongue, hath a revelation, hath an interpretation. Let all things be done unto edifying.
verses.bsb: What then shall we say, brothers? When you come together, everyone has a psalm or a teaching, a revelation, a tongue, or an interpretation. All of these must be done to build up the church.

verses.esv: If any speak in a tongue, let there be only two or at most three, and each in turn, and let someone interpret.
verses.kjv: If any man speak in an unknown tongue, let it be by two, or at the most by three, and that by course; and let one interpret.
verses.bsb: If anyone speaks in a tongue, two, or at most three, should speak in turn, and someone must interpret.

verses.esv: But if there is no one to interpret, let each of them keep silent in church and speak to himself and to God.
verses.kjv: But if there be no interpreter, let him keep silence in the church; and let him speak to himself, and to God.
verses.bsb: But if there is no interpreter, he should remain silent in the church and speak only to himself and God.

verses.esv: Let two or three prophets speak, and let the others weigh what is said.
verses.kjv: Let the prophets speak two or three, and let the other judge.
verses.bsb: Two or three prophets should speak, and the others should weigh carefully what is said.

verses.esv: If a revelation is made to another sitting there, let the first be silent.
verses.kjv: If any thing be revealed to another that sitteth by, let the first hold his peace.
verses.bsb: And if a revelation comes to someone who is seated, the first speaker should stop.

verses.esv: For you can all prophesy one by one, so that all may learn and all be encouraged,
verses.kjv: For ye may all prophesy one by one, that all may learn, and all may be comforted.
verses.bsb: For you can all prophesy in turn so that everyone may be instructed and encouraged.

verses.esv: and the spirits of prophets are subject to prophets.
verses.kjv: And the spirits of the prophets are subject to the prophets.
verses.bsb: The spirits of prophets are subject to prophets.

verses.esv: For God is not a God of confusion but of peace. As in all the churches of the saints,
verses.kjv: For God is not the author of confusion, but of peace, as in all churches of the saints.
verses.bsb: For God is not a God of disorder, but of peace—as in all the churches of the saints.

verses.esv: the women should keep silent in the churches. For they are not permitted to speak, but should be in submission, as the Law also says.
verses.kjv: Let your women keep silence in the churches: for it is not permitted unto them to speak; but they are commanded to be under obedience, as also saith the law.
verses.bsb: Women are to be silent in the churches. They are not permitted to speak, but must be in submission, as the law says.

verses.esv: If there is anything they desire to learn, let them ask their husbands at home. For it is shameful for a woman to speak in church.
verses.kjv: And if they will learn any thing, let them ask their husbands at home: for it is a shame for women to speak in the church.
verses.bsb: If they wish to inquire about something, they are to ask their own husbands at home; for it is dishonorable for a woman to speak in the church.

verses.esv: Or was it from you that the word of God came? Or are you the only ones it has reached?
verses.kjv: What? came the word of God out from you? or came it unto you only?
verses.bsb: Did the word of God originate with you? Or are you the only ones it has reached?

verses.esv: If anyone thinks that he is a prophet, or spiritual, he should acknowledge that the things I am writing to you are a command of the Lord.
verses.kjv: If any man think himself to be a prophet, or spiritual, let him acknowledge that the things that I write unto you are the commandments of the Lord.
verses.bsb: If anyone considers himself a prophet or spiritual person, let him acknowledge that what I am writing you is the Lord’s command.

verses.esv: If anyone does not recognize this, he is not recognized.
verses.kjv: But if any man be ignorant, let him be ignorant.
verses.bsb: But if anyone ignores this, he himself will be ignored.

verses.esv: So, my brothers, earnestly desire to prophesy, and do not forbid speaking in tongues.
verses.kjv: Wherefore, brethren, covet to prophesy, and forbid not to speak with tongues.
verses.bsb: So, my brothers, be eager to prophesy, and do not forbid speaking in tongues.

verses.esv: But all things should be done decently and in order.
verses.kjv: Let all things be done decently and in order.
verses.bsb: But everything must be done in a proper and orderly manner.

verses.esv: Now I would remind you, brothers, of the gospel I preached to you, which you received, in which you stand,
verses.kjv: MOREOVER, brethren, I declare unto you the gospel which I preached unto you, which also ye have received, and wherein ye stand;
verses.bsb: Now, brothers, I want to remind you of the gospel I preached to you, which you received, and in which you stand firm.

verses.esv: and by which you are being saved, if you hold fast to the word I preached to you—unless you believed in vain.
verses.kjv: By which also ye are saved, if ye keep in memory what I preached unto you, unless ye have believed in vain.
verses.bsb: By this gospel you are saved, if you hold firmly to the word I preached to you. Otherwise, you have believed in vain.

verses.esv: For I delivered to you as of first importance what I also received: that Christ died for our sins in accordance with the Scriptures,
verses.kjv: For I delivered unto you first of all that which I also received, how that Christ died for our sins according to the scriptures;
verses.bsb: For what I received I passed on to you as of first importance: that Christ died for our sins according to the Scriptures,

verses.esv: that he was buried, that he was raised on the third day in accordance with the Scriptures,
verses.kjv: And that he was buried, and that he rose again the third day according to the scriptures:
verses.bsb: that He was buried, that He was raised on the third day according to the Scriptures,

verses.esv: and that he appeared to Cephas, then to the twelve.
verses.kjv: And that he was seen of Cephas, then of the twelve:
verses.bsb: and that He appeared to Cephas and then to the Twelve.

verses.esv: Then he appeared to more than five hundred brothers at one time, most of whom are still alive, though some have fallen asleep.
verses.kjv: After that, he was seen of above five hundred brethren at once; of whom the greater part remain unto this present, but some are fallen asleep.
verses.bsb: After that, He appeared to more than five hundred brothers at once, most of whom are still living, though some have fallen asleep.

verses.esv: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
verses.kjv: After that, he was seen of James; then of all the apostles.
verses.bsb: Then He appeared to James, then to all the apostles.

verses.esv: Last of all, as to one untimely born, he appeared also to me.
verses.kjv: And last of all he was seen of me also, as of one born out of due time.
verses.bsb: And last of all He appeared to me also, as to one of untimely birth.

verses.esv: For I am the least of the apostles, unworthy to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the church of God.
verses.kjv: For I am the least of the apostles, that am not meet to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the church of God.
verses.bsb: For I am the least of the apostles and am unworthy to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the church of God.

verses.esv: But by the grace of God I am what I am, and his grace toward me was not in vain. On the contrary, I worked harder than any of them, though it was not I, but the grace of God that is with me.
verses.kjv: But by the grace of God I am what I am: and his grace which was bestowed upon me was not in vain; but I laboured more abundantly than they all: yet not I, but the grace of God which was with me.
verses.bsb: But by the grace of God I am what I am, and His grace to me was not in vain. No, I worked harder than all of them—yet not I, but the grace of God that was with me.

verses.esv: Whether then it was I or they, so we preach and so you believed.
verses.kjv: Therefore whether it were I or they, so we preach, and so ye believed.
verses.bsb: Whether, then, it was I or they, this is what we preach, and this is what you believed.

verses.esv: Now if Christ is proclaimed as raised from the dead, how can some of you say that there is no resurrection of the dead?
verses.kjv: Now if Christ be preached that he rose from the dead, how say some among you that there is no resurrection of the dead?
verses.bsb: But if it is preached that Christ has been raised from the dead, how can some of you say that there is no resurrection of the dead?

verses.esv: But if there is no resurrection of the dead, then not even Christ has been raised.
verses.kjv: But if there be no resurrection of the dead, then is Christ not risen:
verses.bsb: If there is no resurrection of the dead, then not even Christ has been raised.

verses.esv: And if Christ has not been raised, then our preaching is in vain and your faith is in vain.
verses.kjv: And if Christ be not risen, then is our preaching vain, and your faith is also vain.
verses.bsb: And if Christ has not been raised, our preaching is worthless, and so is your faith.

verses.esv: We are even found to be misrepresenting God, because we testified about God that he raised Christ, whom he did not raise if it is true that the dead are not raised.
verses.kjv: Yea, and we are found false witnesses of God; because we have testified of God that he raised up Christ: whom he raised not up, if so be that the dead rise not.
verses.bsb: In that case, we are also exposed as false witnesses about God. For we have testified about God that He raised Christ from the dead, but He did not raise Him if in fact the dead are not raised.

verses.esv: For if the dead are not raised, not even Christ has been raised.
verses.kjv: For if the dead rise not, then is not Christ raised:
verses.bsb: For if the dead are not raised, then not even Christ has been raised.

verses.esv: And if Christ has not been raised, your faith is futile and you are still in your sins.
verses.kjv: And if Christ be not raised, your faith is vain; ye are yet in your sins.
verses.bsb: And if Christ has not been raised, your faith is futile; you are still in your sins.

verses.esv: Then those also who have fallen asleep in Christ have perished.
verses.kjv: Then they also which are fallen asleep in Christ are perished.
verses.bsb: Then those also who have fallen asleep in Christ have perished.

verses.esv: If in Christ we have hope in this life only, we are of all people most to be pitied.
verses.kjv: If in this life only we have hope in Christ, we are of all men most miserable.
verses.bsb: If our hope in Christ is for this life alone, we are to be pitied more than all men.

verses.esv: But in fact Christ has been raised from the dead, the firstfruits of those who have fallen asleep.
verses.kjv: But now is Christ risen from the dead, and become the firstfruits of them that slept.
verses.bsb: But Christ has indeed been raised from the dead, the firstfruits of those who have fallen asleep.

verses.esv: For as by a man came death, by a man has come also the resurrection of the dead.
verses.kjv: For since by man came death, by man came also the resurrection of the dead.
verses.bsb: For since death came through a man, the resurrection of the dead comes also through a man.

verses.esv: For as in Adam all die, so also in Christ shall all be made alive.
verses.kjv: For as in Adam all die, even so in Christ shall all be made alive.
verses.bsb: For as in Adam all die, so in Christ all will be made alive.

verses.esv: But each in his own order: Christ the firstfruits, then at his coming those who belong to Christ.
verses.kjv: But every man in his own order: Christ the firstfruits; afterward they that are Christ’s at his coming.
verses.bsb: But each in his own turn: Christ the firstfruits; then at His coming, those who belong to Him.

verses.esv: Then comes the end, when he delivers the kingdom to God the Father after destroying every rule and every authority and power.
verses.kjv: Then cometh the end, when he shall have delivered up the kingdom to God, even the Father; when he shall have put down all rule and all authority and power.
verses.bsb: Then the end will come, when He hands over the kingdom to God the Father after He has destroyed all dominion, authority, and power.

verses.esv: For he must reign until he has put all his enemies under his feet.
verses.kjv: For he must reign, till he hath put all enemies under his feet.
verses.bsb: For He must reign until He has put all His enemies under His feet.

verses.esv: The last enemy to be destroyed is death.
verses.kjv: The last enemy that shall be destroyed is death.
verses.bsb: The last enemy to be destroyed is death.

verses.esv: For “God has put all things in subjection under his feet.” But when it says, “all things are put in subjection,” it is plain that he is excepted who put all things in subjection under him.
verses.kjv: For he hath put all things under his feet. But when he saith, all things are put under him, it is manifest that he is excepted, which did put all things under him.
verses.bsb: For “God has put everything under His feet.” Now when it says that everything has been put under Him, this clearly does not include the One who put everything under Him.

verses.esv: When all things are subjected to him, then the Son himself will also be subjected to him who put all things in subjection under him, that God may be all in all.
verses.kjv: And when all things shall be subdued unto him, then shall the Son also himself be subject unto him that put all things under him, that God may be all in all.
verses.bsb: And when all things have been subjected to Him, then the Son Himself will be made subject to Him who put all things under Him, so that God may be all in all.

verses.esv: Otherwise, what do people mean by being baptized on behalf of the dead? If the dead are not raised at all, why are people baptized on their behalf?
verses.kjv: Else what shall they do which are baptized for the dead, if the dead rise not at all? why are they then baptized for the dead?
verses.bsb: If these things are not so, what will those do who are baptized for the dead? If the dead are not raised at all, why are people baptized for them?

verses.esv: Why are we in danger every hour?
verses.kjv: And why stand we in jeopardy every hour?
verses.bsb: And why do we endanger ourselves every hour?

verses.esv: I protest, brothers, by my pride in you, which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord, I die every day!
verses.kjv: I protest by your rejoicing which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord, I die daily.
verses.bsb: I face death every day, brothers, as surely as I boast about you in Christ Jesus our Lord.

verses.esv: What do I gain if, humanly speaking, I fought with beasts at Ephesus? If the dead are not raised, “Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die.”
verses.kjv: If after the manner of men I have fought with beasts at Ephesus, what advantageth it me, if the dead rise not? let us eat and drink; for to morrow we die.
verses.bsb: If I fought wild beasts in Ephesus for human motives, what did I gain? If the dead are not raised,  “Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die.”

verses.esv: Do not be deceived: “Bad company ruins good morals.”
verses.kjv: Be not deceived: evil communications corrupt good manners.
verses.bsb: Do not be deceived: “Bad company corrupts good character.”

verses.esv: Wake up from your drunken stupor, as is right, and do not go on sinning. For some have no knowledge of God. I say this to your shame.
verses.kjv: Awake to righteousness, and sin not; for some have not the knowledge of God: I speak this to your shame.
verses.bsb: Sober up as you ought, and stop sinning; for some of you are ignorant of God. I say this to your shame.

verses.esv: But someone will ask, “How are the dead raised? With what kind of body do they come?”
verses.kjv: But some man will say, How are the dead raised up? and with what body do they come?
verses.bsb: But someone will ask, “How are the dead raised? With what kind of body will they come?”

verses.esv: You foolish person! What you sow does not come to life unless it dies.
verses.kjv: Thou fool, that which thou sowest is not quickened, except it die:
verses.bsb: You fool! What you sow does not come to life unless it dies.

verses.esv: And what you sow is not the body that is to be, but a bare kernel, perhaps of wheat or of some other grain.
verses.kjv: And that which thou sowest, thou sowest not that body that shall be, but bare grain, it may chance of wheat, or of some other grain:
verses.bsb: And what you sow is not the body that will be, but just a seed, perhaps of wheat or something else.

verses.esv: But God gives it a body as he has chosen, and to each kind of seed its own body.
verses.kjv: But God giveth it a body as it hath pleased him, and to every seed his own body.
verses.bsb: But God gives it a body as He has designed, and to each kind of seed He gives its own body.

verses.esv: For not all flesh is the same, but there is one kind for humans, another for animals, another for birds, and another for fish.
verses.kjv: All flesh is not the same flesh: but there is one kind of flesh of men, another flesh of beasts, another of fishes, and another of birds.
verses.bsb: Not all flesh is the same: Men have one kind of flesh, animals have another, birds another, and fish another.

verses.esv: There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies, but the glory of the heavenly is of one kind, and the glory of the earthly is of another.
verses.kjv: There are also celestial bodies, and bodies terrestrial: but the glory of the celestial is one, and the glory of the terrestrial is another.
verses.bsb: There are also heavenly bodies and earthly bodies. But the splendor of the heavenly bodies is of one degree, and the splendor of the earthly bodies is of another.

verses.esv: There is one glory of the sun, and another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars; for star differs from star in glory.
verses.kjv: There is one glory of the sun, and another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars: for one star differeth from another star in glory.
verses.bsb: The sun has one degree of splendor, the moon another, and the stars another; and star differs from star in splendor.

verses.esv: So is it with the resurrection of the dead. What is sown is perishable; what is raised is imperishable.
verses.kjv: So also is the resurrection of the dead. It is sown in corruption; it is raised in incorruption:
verses.bsb: So will it be with the resurrection of the dead: What is sown is perishable; it is raised imperishable.

verses.esv: It is sown in dishonor; it is raised in glory. It is sown in weakness; it is raised in power.
verses.kjv: It is sown in dishonour; it is raised in glory: it is sown in weakness; it is raised in power:
verses.bsb: It is sown in dishonor; it is raised in glory. It is sown in weakness; it is raised in power.

verses.esv: It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. If there is a natural body, there is also a spiritual body.
verses.kjv: It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. There is a natural body, and there is a spiritual body.
verses.bsb: It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. If there is a natural body, there is also a spiritual body.

verses.esv: Thus it is written, “The first man Adam became a living being”; the last Adam became a life-giving spirit.
verses.kjv: And so it is written, The first man Adam was made a living soul; the last Adam was made a quickening spirit.
verses.bsb: So it is written: “The first man Adam became a living being;” the last Adam a life-giving spirit.

verses.esv: But it is not the spiritual that is first but the natural, and then the spiritual.
verses.kjv: Howbeit that was not first which is spiritual, but that which is natural; and afterward that which is spiritual.
verses.bsb: The spiritual, however, was not first, but the natural, and then the spiritual.

verses.esv: The first man was from the earth, a man of dust; the second man is from heaven.
verses.kjv: The first man is of the earth, earthy: the second man is the Lord from heaven.
verses.bsb: The first man was of the dust of the earth, the second man from heaven.

verses.esv: As was the man of dust, so also are those who are of the dust, and as is the man of heaven, so also are those who are of heaven.
verses.kjv: As is the earthy, such are they also that are earthy: and as is the heavenly, such are they also that are heavenly.
verses.bsb: As was the earthly man, so also are those who are of the earth; and as is the heavenly man, so also are those who are of heaven.

verses.esv: Just as we have borne the image of the man of dust, we shall also bear the image of the man of heaven.
verses.kjv: And as we have borne the image of the earthy, we shall also bear the image of the heavenly.
verses.bsb: And just as we have borne the likeness of the earthly man, so also shall we bear the likeness of the heavenly man.

verses.esv: I tell you this, brothers: flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God, nor does the perishable inherit the imperishable.
verses.kjv: Now this I say, brethren, that flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God; neither doth corruption inherit incorruption.
verses.bsb: Now I declare to you, brothers, that flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God, nor does the perishable inherit the imperishable.

verses.esv: Behold! I tell you a mystery. We shall not all sleep, but we shall all be changed,
verses.kjv: Behold, I shew you a mystery; We shall not all sleep, but we shall all be changed,
verses.bsb: Listen, I tell you a mystery: We will not all sleep, but we will all be changed—

verses.esv: in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet. For the trumpet will sound, and the dead will be raised imperishable, and we shall be changed.
verses.kjv: In a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trump: for the trumpet shall sound, and the dead shall be raised incorruptible, and we shall be changed.
verses.bsb: in an instant, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet. For the trumpet will sound, the dead will be raised imperishable, and we will be changed.

verses.esv: For this perishable body must put on the imperishable, and this mortal body must put on immortality.
verses.kjv: For this corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal must put on immortality.
verses.bsb: For the perishable must be clothed with the imperishable, and the mortal with immortality.

verses.esv: When the perishable puts on the imperishable, and the mortal puts on immortality, then shall come to pass the saying that is written: “Death is swallowed up in victory.”
verses.kjv: So when this corruptible shall have put on incorruption, and this mortal shall have put on immortality, then shall be brought to pass the saying that is written, Death is swallowed up in victory.
verses.bsb: When the perishable has been clothed with the imperishable and the mortal with immortality, then the saying that is written will come to pass: “Death has been swallowed up in victory.”

verses.esv: “O death, where is your victory? O death, where is your sting?”
verses.kjv: O death, where is thy sting? O grave, where is thy victory?
verses.bsb: “Where, O Death, is your victory? Where, O Death, is your sting?”

verses.esv: The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin is the law.
verses.kjv: The sting of death is sin; and the strength of sin is the law.
verses.bsb: The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin is the law.

verses.esv: But thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.kjv: But thanks be to God, which giveth us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.bsb: But thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ!

verses.esv: Therefore, my beloved brothers, be steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, knowing that in the Lord your labor is not in vain.
verses.kjv: Therefore, my beloved brethren, be ye stedfast, unmoveable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, forasmuch as ye know that your labour is not in vain in the Lord.
verses.bsb: Therefore, my beloved brothers, be steadfast and immovable. Always excel in the work of the Lord, because you know that your labor in the Lord is not in vain.

verses.esv: Now concerning the collection for the saints: as I directed the churches of Galatia, so you also are to do.
verses.kjv: NOW concerning the collection for the saints, as I have given order to the churches of Galatia, even so do ye.
verses.bsb: Now about the collection for the saints, you are to do as I directed the churches of Galatia:

verses.esv: On the first day of every week, each of you is to put something aside and store it up, as he may prosper, so that there will be no collecting when I come.
verses.kjv: Upon the first day of the week let every one of you lay by him in store, as God hath prospered him, that there be no gatherings when I come.
verses.bsb: On the first day of every week, each of you should set aside a portion of his income, saving it up, so that when I come no collections will be needed.

verses.esv: And when I arrive, I will send those whom you accredit by letter to carry your gift to Jerusalem.
verses.kjv: And when I come, whomsoever ye shall approve by your letters, them will I send to bring your liberality unto Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: Then, on my arrival, I will send letters with those you recommend to carry your gift to Jerusalem.

verses.esv: If it seems advisable that I should go also, they will accompany me.
verses.kjv: And if it be meet that I go also, they shall go with me.
verses.bsb: And if it is advisable for me to go also, they can travel with me.

verses.esv: I will visit you after passing through Macedonia, for I intend to pass through Macedonia,
verses.kjv: Now I will come unto you, when I shall pass through Macedonia: for I do pass through Macedonia.
verses.bsb: After I go through Macedonia, however, I will come to you; for I will be going through Macedonia.

verses.esv: and perhaps I will stay with you or even spend the winter, so that you may help me on my journey, wherever I go.
verses.kjv: And it may be that I will abide, yea, and winter with you, that ye may bring me on my journey whithersoever I go.
verses.bsb: Perhaps I will stay with you awhile, or even spend the winter, so that you can help me on my journey, wherever I go.

verses.esv: For I do not want to see you now just in passing. I hope to spend some time with you, if the Lord permits.
verses.kjv: For I will not see you now by the way; but I trust to tarry a while with you, if the Lord permit.
verses.bsb: For I do not want to see you now only in passing; I hope to spend some time with you, if the Lord permits.

verses.esv: But I will stay in Ephesus until Pentecost,
verses.kjv: But I will tarry at Ephesus until Pentecost.
verses.bsb: But I will stay in Ephesus until Pentecost,

verses.esv: for a wide door for effective work has opened to me, and there are many adversaries.
verses.kjv: For a great door and effectual is opened unto me, and there are many adversaries.
verses.bsb: because a great door for effective work has opened to me, even though many oppose me.

verses.esv: When Timothy comes, see that you put him at ease among you, for he is doing the work of the Lord, as I am.
verses.kjv: Now if Timotheus come, see that he may be with you without fear: for he worketh the work of the Lord, as I also do.
verses.bsb: If Timothy comes, see to it that he has nothing to fear while he is with you, for he is doing the work of the Lord, just as I am.

verses.esv: So let no one despise him. Help him on his way in peace, that he may return to me, for I am expecting him with the brothers.
verses.kjv: Let no man therefore despise him: but conduct him forth in peace, that he may come unto me: for I look for him with the brethren.
verses.bsb: No one, then, should treat him with contempt. Send him on his way in peace so that he can return to me, for I am expecting him along with the brothers.

verses.esv: Now concerning our brother Apollos, I strongly urged him to visit you with the other brothers, but it was not at all his will to come now. He will come when he has opportunity.
verses.kjv: As touching our brother Apollos, I greatly desired him to come unto you with the brethren: but his will was not at all to come at this time; but he will come when he shall have convenient time.
verses.bsb: Now about our brother Apollos: I strongly urged him to go to you with the brothers. He was not at all inclined to go now, but he will go when he has the opportunity.

verses.esv: Be watchful, stand firm in the faith, act like men, be strong.
verses.kjv: Watch ye, stand fast in the faith, quit you like men, be strong.
verses.bsb: Be on the alert. Stand firm in the faith. Be men of courage. Be strong.

verses.esv: Let all that you do be done in love.
verses.kjv: Let all your things be done with charity.
verses.bsb: Do everything in love.

verses.esv: Now I urge you, brothers—you know that the household of Stephanas were the first converts in Achaia, and that they have devoted themselves to the service of the saints—
verses.kjv: I beseech you, brethren, (ye know the house of Stephanas, that it is the firstfruits of Achaia, and that they have addicted themselves to the ministry of the saints,)
verses.bsb: You know that Stephanas and his household were the first converts in Achaia, and they have devoted themselves to the service of the saints. Now I urge you, brothers,

verses.esv: be subject to such as these, and to every fellow worker and laborer.
verses.kjv: That ye submit yourselves unto such, and to every one that helpeth with us, and laboureth.
verses.bsb: to submit to such as these, and to every fellow worker and laborer.

verses.esv: I rejoice at the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus, because they have made up for your absence,
verses.kjv: I am glad of the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus: for that which was lacking on your part they have supplied.
verses.bsb: I am glad that Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus have arrived, because they have supplied what was lacking from you.

verses.esv: for they refreshed my spirit as well as yours. Give recognition to such people.
verses.kjv: For they have refreshed my spirit and yours: therefore acknowledge ye them that are such.
verses.bsb: For they refreshed my spirit and yours as well. Show your appreciation, therefore, to such men.

verses.esv: The churches of Asia send you greetings. Aquila and Prisca, together with the church in their house, send you hearty greetings in the Lord.
verses.kjv: The churches of Asia salute you. Aquila and Priscilla salute you much in the Lord, with the church that is in their house.
verses.bsb: The churches in the province of Asia send you greetings.  Aquila and Prisca greet you warmly in the Lord, and so does the church that meets at their house.

verses.esv: All the brothers send you greetings. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
verses.kjv: All the brethren greet you. Greet ye one another with an holy kiss.
verses.bsb: All the brothers here send you greetings. Greet one another with a holy kiss.

verses.esv: I, Paul, write this greeting with my own hand.
verses.kjv: The salutation of me Paul with mine own hand.
verses.bsb: This greeting is in my own hand—Paul.

verses.esv: If anyone has no love for the Lord, let him be accursed. Our Lord, come!
verses.kjv: If any man love not the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be Anathema Maran-atha.
verses.bsb: If anyone does not love the Lord, let him be under a curse. Come, O Lord!

verses.esv: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
verses.kjv: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
verses.bsb: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.

verses.esv: My love be with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen.
verses.kjv: My love be with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen. ¶ The first epistle to the Corinthians was written from Philippi by Stephanas, and Fortunatus, and Achaicus, and Timotheus. THE
verses.bsb: My love be with all of you in Christ Jesus.  Amen.
